name: loop_slice_profile
description: Loop Slice 1D profile cutter (task 0256: Profile + Inset/depth). Cage = a FLAT open strip of 3 quads in a row along X, 1 quad wide in Z, all at y=0 (planar). Verts (col i, row j) at (x=i, y=0, z=j): 0=(0,0,0) 1=(0,0,1) 2=(1,0,0) 3=(1,0,1) 4=(2,0,0) 5=(2,0,1) 6=(3,0,0) 7=(3,0,1). Faces Q0=[0,2,3,1] Q1=[2,4,5,3] Q2=[4,6,7,5]. Q1 is wound so its geometric normal faceNormal([2,4,5,3])=(0,-1,0)=-Y. The seed edge (1,0,0)-(2,0,0)=(2,4) gives a 1-face open ring cutting Q1's two long rails (2,4) at z=0 and (3,5) at z=1, both running x=1..2. A profile inserts MULTIPLE loops at its along-cut sample fractions and displaces each along the surface normal by height*depth. flat_baseline: no profile, 3 explicit loops at t=[0.25,0.5,0.75] all stay on the surface (y=0) — this is the byte-for-byte multi-loop flat cut the profile default reduces to. vee_depth2: the Vee profile (tent, samples t=[0.25,0.5,0.75] h=[0.5,1.0,0.5]) with Inset depth=2 presses a V into the surface along -Y: y=[-1,-2,-1] (apex -2 at the centre, x=1.5). vee_depth0: the same Vee profile with the reference default Inset=0 leaves every loop on the surface (y=0) — a non-flat profile is a no-op until Inset is raised. Topology is IDENTICAL in every case (14 verts / 19 edges / 6 faces: Q1 -> 4 sub-quads via 3 loops, 6 new rail midpoints) — the profile relocates the new verts only.
source: MECHANISM LIVE-CONFIRMED (task 0263), profile CURVES genuinely opaque. The Inset (`depth`) DISPLACEMENT DIRECTION was captured on the reference app (attribute via command port + a real interactive commit): a flat strip at y=0, seed edge, depth=2 with NO profile places the new loop at (1.5, 2.0, 0) — i.e. the reference insets the loop along the FACE NORMAL by exactly `depth`, confirming vibe3d's mechanism (displace each new loop by height*depth along the rail's averaged face normal; see source/mesh.d insertEdgeLoopsMulti). The reference profile preset LIBRARY is genuinely opaque: the presets are closed-content Bezier-curve files (moveto/curveto paths) in the reference's content library, NOT scalar attrs, and loading one is a preset-browser widget action; the profile sub-controls (Inset/Reverse/Aspect) are ALSO gated behind a loaded profile (same blocking-dialog pattern as Keep Quads). Attempt log: (a) setting the Inset attribute to 2 with no profile -> the normal-inset above (mechanism verified); (b) the built-in flat/round/vee/step curves here remain vibe3d-DEFINED stand-ins (LoopSliceTool.profileSamples) — NO exact-preset-match claim; only the sample->loop->normal-inset mechanism is reference-faithful, now with the inset direction+magnitude live-confirmed. The Vee tent + flat/depth-0 no-op invariants also live in the source/mesh.d kernel unittest.
